page: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Xen hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate performance across standard compute workloads, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the average range. Memory bandwidth for read-only small block operations is measured at 625.85 GB/sec, though write operations on large blocks show weaker throughput at 24.83 GB/sec. The instance exhibits poor performance in single-threaded tasks, as evidenced by a low Geekbench single-core score of 874.0 and slow bzip3 single-threaded decompression. Database operations and software compilation benchmarks show average capabilities, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak.

The t2.2xlarge utilizes a burstable CPU credit model, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads with variable CPU utilization. It allows users to maintain a low baseline cost while retaining the ability to burst to full CPU performance when required. However, the tradeoff lies in the potential exhaustion of CPU credits during prolonged high-intensity tasks, which throttles performance to baseline levels. Additionally, the lack of local storage requires reliance on external storage volumes, and the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth may limit data-intensive transfers.

This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads with bursty CPU demands, such as development environments, code compilation, low-to-medium traffic web servers, and small database operations that do not require sustained high-throughput compute or memory performance.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance features a 2.3 GHz clock speed and a 45 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for multi-core CPU tasks, software compilation, and database operations, though it exhibits weaker results in single-threaded operations, write memory bandwidth, and static web serving. Utilizing a burstable CPU allocation model, the t2.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. It is highly suitable for development environments, code repositories, low-traffic web servers, and non-production database workloads that benefit from burst capacity without requiring continuous high-performance compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed, designed for workloads with variable CPU demands.

tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance with eight cores and thirty-two gigabytes of memory, designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization.

Burstable CPU allocation model for handling variable workloads cost-effectively
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks and software compilation
